title: The two-point trapezoidal rule is additive

source.citation: Alfio Quarteroni, Riccardo Sacco, and Fausto Saleri, Numerical Mathematics, 2nd ed., Springer, 2007, Ch. 9 (Numerical Integration).
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: The one-cell trapezoidal rule applied to endpointwise-added data equals the sum of the two trapezoidal-rule values.
assumptions: two endpoint sample pairs are given

declarations.name: trapezoid2
declarations.statement: def trapezoid2 (h f0 f1 : Real) : Real := h * (f0 + f1) / 2

declarations.name: trapezoid2_add
declarations.statement: theorem trapezoid2_add (h f0 f1 g0 g1 : Real) : trapezoid2 h (f0 + g0) (f1 + g1) = trapezoid2 h f0 f1 + trapezoid2 h g0 g1

tasks.input: Translate the quadrature theorem `trapezoid2_add` into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem trapezoid2_add (h f0 f1 g0 g1 : Real) : trapezoid2 h (f0 + g0) (f1 + g1) = trapezoid2 h f0 f1 + trapezoid2 h g0 g1

tasks.input: Identify the main algebraic or order lemma needed for the quadrature proof.
